lesson_title: Complex Texts, Literature and Culture: Listening Lesson 4
learning_objective: Learners can critique, refine, interpret nuance and produce expert-level responses in a listening task about abstract argument.
cefr_outcome: Learners can understand virtually everything heard or read and express themselves precisely in complex situations.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise listening for complex texts, literature and culture. You will learn useful language connected to abstract argument and practise register control. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at C2 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: register control. Vocabulary theme: abstract argument. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At C2, learners should critique, refine, interpret nuance and produce expert-level responses with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use register control to talk about abstract argument.
Example 2: The learner gives a C2 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for C2.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using register control.
options: Correct use of register control in a C2 sentence.
Incorrect word order with register control.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses register control accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of register control.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the abstract argument phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand abstract argument.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand abstract argument.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form register control. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about abstract argument using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: C2 Listening Check - Complex Texts, Literature and Culture
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short listening task using register control and vocabulary from abstract argument.
success_criteria: Uses register control with reasonable accuracy
Uses vocabulary connected to abstract argument
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses register control and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to abstract argument.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about abstract argument. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short C2 response about abstract argument. Include the target language: register control.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for C2-M02-L04. Speaker one: Today we are practising abstract argument. Speaker two: I need to use register control correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Register Control form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for register control
